content:
Let’s place the rectangle so that  
A=(0,0), B=(5,0), D=(0,12), C=(5,12).  
Then AB is the x–axis and AD is the y–axis.  The point E must lie a distance 12 from y=0 and 1 from x=0, and “outside” the rectangle; the only choice giving ΔABE acute is  
E=(1,−12).  

We seek a line through E which meets AB at F=(t,0) and CD (the line y=12) at G=(x_G,12), and which cuts the 5×12 rectangle into two regions of area 30 each.  The slope of EF is  
m = (0−(−12))/(t−1)=12/(t−1),  
so its intersection with y=12 satisfies  
12+12 = m(x_G−1) ⇒ 24 = [12/(t−1)]·(x_G−1) ⇒ x_G =1+2(t−1)=2t−1.  

Thus  
F=(t,0),  G=(2t−1,12),  
and   
FG²=( (2t−1)−t )² +12² = (t−1)² +144.  

The line cuts off the quadrilateral F→B→C→G whose shoelace‐area must be 30:  
Area =½|120 −(36t−12)| =½|132−36t| =30  
⇒ |132−36t|=60 ⇒ t=2  (the only admissible root).  

Hence F=(2,0), G=(3,12) and  
FG²=(3−2)²+12²=1+144=145.  

Answer: \boxed{145}.
